clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection with organ dysfunction. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) suggests coagulopathy, while AKI Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) indicates severe kidney injury. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) further support systemic inflammation.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine rose 6.5 mg/dL in 6 hours (Stage 3 AKI), exceeding KDIGO criteria
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation score threshold
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused workup
Start renal replacement therapy evaluation for Stage 3 AKI
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Assess for source control (imaging, surgical consult)
doctor_handoff: Review for sepsis source identification, response to initial antimicrobials, and AKI progression. Verify vasopressor requirements for septic shock confirmation.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 3 (Creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) with persistent thrombocytopenia

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
